_meta.provenance: Same 13-point real-arm dataset as v1. Re-processed by the T7A v2 calibrator (PR phase-2b-v2-calibrate). Numbers unchanged from v1; this bump records (a) the new calibrator capability and (b) that v2's polygon fit was attempted and intentionally NOT deployed because the v1 capture's coupling-probe data underestimates the true boundary (see _coupling_note). Polygon deployment deferred to v2 (T7B re-probe).

_provenance.annulus_inner: calibrate_bounds v2, 20mm margin from observed min planar radius
_provenance.annulus_outer: calibrate_bounds v2, clamped at spec reach 440mm. Real arm reached ρ≈436mm at outer_left45.
_provenance.z_min: observed at floor_0 (J2=82.8, J3=77.3, J4=81.2), bare flange touching tabletop. Future tools: z_min_effective = z_min + tool.z_offset_mm.
_provenance.z_max: observed only — not explicitly probed. Real arm can likely go higher (spec workspace ~280mm above origin).
_provenance.j1_rear_dead_zone: now derived from data (was placeholder-capped at 40° in v1). 2 × (180° − max(|J1|)) = 2 × (180° − 159.9°) = 40.2°.

_provenance.note: v2 calibrator applies spec-fallback automatically for un-probed sides. J3 upper is kept at the observed coupling-limited value 77.3° (NOT the spec 105°) for the same reason coupling polygon is deferred: without a trusted polygon, per-axis J3 spec would over-permit (J2=0, J3=90 would pass even though real arm alarms ~55).
_provenance.J1: both ends intentionally pushed to limit during outer_left135/right135
_provenance.J2_lower: spec fallback (observed min -14.7°, 10.3° from spec → not trusted)
_provenance.J3_upper: MANUAL OVERRIDE — kept at v1 observed cap 77.3° because no trusted coupling polygon. Calibrator suggests 105° (spec) but that's only safe when paired with a polygon.
_provenance.J3_lower: spec fallback (real arm not pushed to J3 lower limit)
_provenance.J4: spec fallback — J4 was not exercised (random subset)

_coupling_note.v2_algorithm_ready: calibrate_bounds v2 (T7A) can FIT a 2-segment piecewise envelope from coupling data: select_coupling_points → filter_masquerading_points (J2_cutoff=50°, z_proximity=30mm) → fit_piecewise_envelope. Running it on the v1 capture produces coup_rising / coup_flat (see outputs/calibrated_bounds_*.json for the result).
_coupling_note.why_not_deployed_from_v1_data: The fitted polygon rejects the factory pose (J2=0, J3≈60) which is a known-safe stable arm position (PROGRESS finding 4 / calibration pair A1). The v1 coupling probe points captured operator-judged J3 maxima of 50.4 (at J2=-7.4) and 55.3 (at J2=+14.1) — conservative boundaries that mathematically rule out the home pose. Either the probe technique was conservative (stopped before true alarm) or the alarm behavior differs between jog-into-limit vs static-hold, but either way: deploying a polygon that rejects home would block every Phase 5 motion from starting.
next_step_T7B: Re-probe coupling points with explicit 'push J3 until alarm fires' protocol (vs operator judgment).
Capture J2 ∈ {-15, -10, -5, 0, +5, +10, +20, +30, +40} sweep at safe mid-z height to avoid table interference.
Sanity test: fitted polygon must accept factory pose (J2=0, J3=60) and reject known-alarm poses.
Then deploy the polygon to this config.
_coupling_note.v1_protection_gap_in_meantime: Without polygon, safety only enforces per-axis J3 ≤ 77.3° (the observed coupling-limited max at floor_0). Some (J2, J3) combinations will pass safety but alarm at controller mid-motion — known limitation, controller's own safety catches without hardware damage.

_future_tool_offset.note: Phase 6 controller will add tool transforms. z_min is currently for bare flange.
